lastCarrier: Ravn Alaska (final scheduled ANC service Aug 2025; Aleutian Airways now serves route)

lostDestinations.source: Alaska Public Media, Aug 15 2025: 'Regional airline Ravn Alaska calls it quits'

detail: Ravn Alaska halted Homer service March 2025 and all operations August 5, 2025 (Float Alaska parent filed Ch.11 Jan 2026); Aleutian Airways subsequently launched twice-daily ENA-ANC service June 2025.
source: Alaska Public Media Aug 15 2025; AviationSourceNews 'Aleutian Airways Expands in Alaska After Ravn Air Shutdown'

lastCarrier: Aleutian Airways (current sole commercial carrier after Ravn exit)

lostDestinations.source: KBBI / Alaska's News Source, March 5-6 2025: 'Ravn Alaska stops Homer flights'

detail: Ravn Alaska discontinued Homer service March 3, 2025 citing inability to reach agreement with the airport, then shut down entirely August 5, 2025; Aleutian Airways remains the only commercial operator.
source: KBBI Mar 5 2025; ADN Aug 14 2025 'Ravn Alaska shuts down flights'

lastCarrier: Reeve Air Alaska (EAS contract, awarded fall 2025 after Ravn shutdown)

lostDestinations.source: AVgeekery 'The Final Flight: Ravn Alaska's Quiet Exit' — last Ravn flight VDZ-ANC Aug 5 2025

detail: Ravn Alaska's final ever flight was the VDZ-ANC leg on August 5, 2025; the city subsequently secured an EAS contract awarded to Reeve Air Alaska in fall 2025. Arctic Legacy also operates daily service.
source: AVgeekery Aug 2025; City of Valdez press release Dec 6 2024 (DOT EAS selection); ADN Aug 14 2025

lastCarrier: Alaska Airlines (EAS, daily ANC service; also continues Juneau milk-run)

lostDestinations.source: Alaska Public Media Apr 7 2020: 'Carriers scramble to fill void left by Ravn bankruptcy'

detail: Ravn/PenAir served Cordova until April 2020 bankruptcy; Alaska Airlines stepped in under EAS and now holds a contract through April 2027 worth $17.8M annually for Cordova, Yakutat, Gustavus, Petersburg, and Wrangell.
source: Cordova Times May 29 2025 'Essential Air Service contract secured by Alaska Airlines covers Cordova'; DOT EAS subsidy report Mar 2024

lastCarrier: Alaska Airlines (stepped in 2020 after Ravn bankruptcy; current EAS provider)

lostDestinations.source: KDLG Jan 14 2022: 'After less than a year of service, Ravn Alaska halts flights to Dillingham again'

detail: PenAir served Dillingham as its core Alaska route before being absorbed by Ravn (2019); Ravn halted all service April 2020 (Ch.11 bankruptcy), briefly restored Dillingham service in 2021, then halted it again January 2022; Alaska Airlines currently provides EAS service.
source: KDLG Jan 14 2022; Alaska Business Magazine June 2019 'Ravn Air Picks Up PenAir'; ADN Apr 5 2020

lastCarrier: Alaska Airlines (EAS; current provider after Ravn/PenAir collapse)

lostDestinations.source: Wikipedia PenAir: PenAir operations ceased 2020 after RavnAir Group filed Ch.11 bankruptcy April 2020

detail: PenAir served King Salmon as a core Alaska EAS route; after the 2017 PenAir Ch.11 filing the airline was sold to Ravn's owners and absorbed by 2019; all service ceased April 2020 with RavnAir Group bankruptcy; Alaska Airlines now holds the EAS contract.
source: PenAir Wikipedia; ADN Apr 5 2020; AirlineGeeks Aug 8 2017 'PenAir Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy'

lastCarrier: Alaska Airlines (EAS contract through Sep 2027, 2x weekly Boeing 737)

lostDestinations.source: Grokipedia / ONEMILEATATIME: Reeve Aleutian ceased all operations 2000 after decades of Adak 727 service

detail: Reeve Aleutian Airways served Adak from the late 1940s through 2000 with DC-3s, Electras, and 727s; after Reeve's collapse the US government subsidizes Alaska Airlines at >$2M/year (~$10K per one-way flight) to maintain the sole lifeline for ~300 residents.
source: ONEMILEATATIME 'Alaska Airlines Route To Adak Via Cold Bay'; DOT Subsidized EAS report AK Mar 2024

lastCarrier: Alaska Airlines (EAS, seasonal summer jet service JNU; small carriers year-round)

lostDestinations.source: Alaska Airlines airport page GST; DOT EAS award through Apr 2025 for up to 14 weekly flights summer

detail: Gustavus has no road connection to any hub; EAS subsidizes seasonal Alaska Airlines Boeing 737 service to Juneau (early June–late August); year-round connectivity depends on small unsubsidized regional carriers operating much smaller aircraft.
source: Alaska Airlines GST airport info; AirlineGeeks EAS Round Up Part 4, Jun 2021; DOT Subsidized EAS AK Mar 2024

lastCarrier: Mokulele Airlines (Southern Airways Express; small Cessna Caravan service to OGG/HNL)

lostDestinations.source: Honolulu Star-Advertiser Jan 6 2021: ''Ohana by Hawaiian to suspend air service between Honolulu and both Molokai and Lanai'
Island Air Wikipedia: discontinued Ho'olehua Molokai service 2014

detail: Ohana by Hawaiian suspended Molokai service January 14, 2021 and permanently ended May 27, 2021; replacement Mokulele Airlines uses 9-seat Cessna Caravans vs. the prior 48-seat ATR-72, a steep capacity cut affecting kupuna and disabled residents traveling for medical care.
source: Honolulu Civil Beat May 2021 'Ohana Shutdown Leaves Molokai, Lanai Residents With Limited Flight Options'; Star-Advertiser Jan 6 2021

lastCarrier: Mokulele Airlines (current scheduled service; Lanai Air charter also operates)

lostDestinations.source: Star-Advertiser Jan 6 2021 / Maui News Jan 2021: Ohana suspended Lanai service Jan 14 2021, permanently ended May 27 2021
Lanai Airport Wikipedia: Island Air ended Lanai service March 31 2016

detail: Island Air ended Lanai scheduled service March 31, 2016; Ohana by Hawaiian filled the gap from 2014 but permanently ceased Lanai operations May 27, 2021; Mokulele Airlines now provides the only scheduled passenger service.
source: Lanai Airport Wikipedia; Star-Advertiser May 27 2021; AirlineGeeks May 28 2021 'Hawaiian Airlines Terminates Ohana Regional Subsidiary'

lostDestinations.source: Hawaiian Airlines Newsroom Mar 1 2017 (service began); Simple Flying May 2021 'End Of An Era: Hawaiian Airlines Cuts Turboprop Ohana Services'
Ohana by Hawaiian Wikipedia: routes included HNL and OGG to JHM

detail: Ohana by Hawaiian launched JHM service March 1, 2017 with ATR 42-500 aircraft; service was suspended March 25, 2020 for COVID and permanently terminated May 27, 2021 when Hawaiian ended the Ohana brand; only on-demand charter flights (Big Island Air, Royal Pacific Air) operate there now.
source: Hawaiian Airlines Newsroom release 2017-03-01; Maui Now May 27 2021 'Hawaiian Airlines Ends Ohana by Hawaiian Service'; Kapalua Airport Wikipedia

lostDestinations.source: Waimea-Kohala Airport Wikipedia: large jet service by Aloha/Hawaiian ended early 2000s when both airlines withdrew DC-9/BAC-111 service

detail: Waimea-Kohala once received DC-9 and BAC One-Eleven jet service from Hawaiian Airlines and Aloha Airlines to Honolulu and Kahului; both majors withdrew in the early 2000s and the airport reoriented to small commuter operations; only Mokulele Airlines (twice-daily Kahului) remains as of 2016+.
source: Waimea-Kohala Airport Wikipedia (service history section); Hawaii Aviation archive
